metadata.description: HarnessFlow — From idea to shipped product: high-quality engineering workflows for AI agents. Spec-anchored SDD, gated TDD, evidence-based routing, independent reviews, and formal closeout.

plugins.name: harness-flow
plugins.description: HarnessFlow skill pack: 24 hf-* skills covering discovery -> spec -> design -> tasks -> TDD -> reviews -> gates -> finalize -> verify (hf-browser-testing, v0.2.0) -> release (hf-release, v0.4.0), with evidence-based routing. v0.3.0 adds Cursor as the third officially-supported client (Claude Code + OpenCode + Cursor); v0.4.0 introduces hf-release as a standalone release-tier skill decoupled from the router (engineer-level release; deployment / staged rollout / monitoring / rollback remain deferred). v0.5.0 adds a closeout HTML companion report to hf-finalize. v0.5.1 patches a vendoring bug by moving render-closeout-html.py into skills/hf-finalize/scripts/ (HF skill anatomy v2: 4 subdirs SKILL.md + references/ + evals/ + scripts/, per ADR-006). v0.6.0 introduces install.sh + uninstall.sh + tests/test_install_scripts.sh at the repo root (per ADR-007 + ADR-008): a one-command bash installer for vendoring HF into any host repository, covering Cursor / OpenCode / both targets x copy / symlink topologies = 6 combinations + 2 negative-path scenarios = 14 e2e PASS; manifest-based uninstall preserves user-added skills (HYP-002 Blocking validated); pure bash 3.2+ compatible with zero new runtime dependencies (no jq/python/node/npm). Skill set stays at 24 hf-* + using-hf-workflow; slash commands stay at 7; hf-skill anatomy v2 stays unchanged (install scripts live at repo root, not under skills/<name>/scripts/, per ADR-008 D8). Still pre-release: deployment / staged rollout / monitoring / rollback remain on the v0.7+ roadmap; 4 remaining clients (Gemini CLI / Windsurf / GitHub Copilot / Kiro) and 3 personas continue deferred.
